cards.prompt: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.
cards.explanation: CPU is short for Central Processing Unit, the main processor of a computer.
www stands for World Wide Web, the system of web pages on the internet.

cards.prompt: Email addresses usually contain the '@' symbol.
cards.explanation: An email address places the '@' symbol between the user name and the domain.

cards.prompt: Wi-Fi lets devices connect to the internet without cables.
cards.explanation: Wi-Fi uses radio signals to connect devices to a network without wires.
1 GB is about 1,000 MB, so it is much larger than 1 MB.

cards.prompt: Android and iOS are operating systems for smartphones.
cards.explanation: Android (Google) and iOS (Apple) are the two most widely used smartphone operating systems.

cards.prompt: RAM keeps all its data even after the computer is turned off.
cards.explanation: RAM is volatile memory; its contents are lost when the power is off.
HTML is a markup language used to build web pages, not an antivirus program.

cards.prompt: One byte is smaller than one bit.
cards.explanation: One byte equals eight bits, so a byte is larger than a bit.

cards.prompt: iOS is an operating system made by Google.
cards.explanation: iOS is made by Apple; Google makes Android.

cards.prompt: A 'URL' is a type of computer virus.
cards.explanation: A URL is a web address that points to a page or file, not a virus.

cards.prompt: The internet was invented in the year 1500.
cards.explanation: The internet grew out of networks developed in the 20th century, not in 1500.
